_note: Per-lesson role-play scenario. Grounded in B1/003 dialogue + vocab. Learner plays Sam; computer plays Lily. Each NPC node offers 2-3 branches so the same lesson language can be reached different ways. Consumed by RP.Engine + RP.classifiers.local (see roleplay/).
title: 003 · My Favorite Book

routes.meaning: Sam has not read it and asks what it is about.
examples: No, not yet. What is it about?
No, not yet. What's it about?
Not yet. What is it about?
No, I haven't. What is it about?
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “No, not yet. What is it about?”

routes.meaning: Sam asks the name of the book.
examples: No, what is the name of the book?
routes.hint: 💬 You can ask “What is it called?” or “No, not yet. What is it about?”

partial.message: Tell Lily and ask more: “No, not yet. What is it about?”

off_topic.message: Lily asked about a book. Say “No, not yet. What is it about?”

npc.text: It's a famous fantasy story. Ask me what it's about!

routes.meaning: Sam asks what it is about.
examples: No, not yet. What is it about?
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “No, not yet. What is it about?”

npc.text: It's about a boy who discovers a magic world.

routes.meaning: Sam says it sounds interesting and asks if it's good.
examples: That sounds interesting. Is it good?
That sounds cool. Is it good?
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “That sounds interesting. Is it good?”

routes.meaning: Sam reacts with excitement about the magic world.
examples: Wow, a magic world sounds amazing!
A magic world? That's so cool!
routes.hint: 💬 You can react, or ask “That sounds interesting. Is it good?”

partial.message: Ask Lily's opinion: “That sounds interesting. Is it good?”

off_topic.message: Ask about the book: “That sounds interesting. Is it good?”

npc.text: Yes, there are dragons and secret doors!

routes.meaning: Sam asks if the book is good.
examples: That sounds interesting. Is it good?
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “That sounds interesting. Is it good?”

npc.text: Yes, I think it's the best book I have ever read.

routes.meaning: Sam asks why she likes it so much.
examples: Why do you like it so much?
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Why do you like it so much?”

routes.meaning: Sam asks if the book is long.
routes.hint: 💬 You can ask “Is it a long book?” or “Why do you like it so much?”

partial.message: Ask for her reason: “Why do you like it so much?”

off_topic.message: Ask Lily “Why do you like it so much?”

npc.text: It's not too long, and every page is exciting.

routes.meaning: Sam asks why she likes it.
examples: Why do you like it so much?
So why do you like it so much?
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Why do you like it so much?”

partial.message: Ask “Why do you like it so much?”

off_topic.message: Ask Lily “Why do you like it so much?”

npc.text: Because the story is exciting, although the ending is sad.

routes.meaning: Sam prefers funny stories but will try this one.
examples: I prefer funny stories, but I will try it.
I prefer funny stories, but I'll try it.
I like funny stories more, but I will try it.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “I prefer funny stories, but I will try it.”

routes.meaning: Sam asks about the sad ending.
routes.hint: 💬 You can ask “Why is the ending sad?” or say “I prefer funny stories, but I will try it.”

partial.message: Share what you like: “I prefer funny stories, but I will try it.”

off_topic.message: Tell Lily “I prefer funny stories, but I will try it.”

npc.text: The ending is sad, but it makes you think.

routes.meaning: Sam prefers funny stories but will try this one.
examples: I prefer funny stories, but I will try it.
I usually like funny stories, but I will try it.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “I prefer funny stories, but I will try it.”

partial.message: Say “I prefer funny stories, but I will try it.”

npc.text: You should read it. I'm sure you will enjoy it.

routes.meaning: Sam asks to borrow the book.
examples: Sure, can I borrow the book?
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Okay, can I borrow it?”

routes.meaning: Sam says he'll read it later.
examples: I'll find it at the library.
routes.hint: 💬 You can say “Okay, can I borrow it?” or “Maybe I'll read it later.”

partial.message: Ask to borrow it: “Okay, can I borrow it?”

off_topic.message: Lily says you'll enjoy it. Ask “Okay, can I borrow it?”

npc.text: Of course! Here you go. Tell me what you think!
No rush! It will be waiting for you. Happy reading!
